title: sairocarpus cornutus subsp. cornutus
intro: Sairocarpus cornutus subsp. cornutus, also known as the horned toadpod or horned Sairocarpus, is a plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Sairocarpus cornutus subsp. cornutus include its use in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves and roots are commonly used in herbal remedies.
